Title: A Maize Gene Regulatory Network for Phenolic Metabolism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall structure and mechanical support
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: key late lignin-branch gene heavily targeted by TFs in the GRN
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter-centered Y1H; ChIP-qPCR for selected TFs; integrated ChIP-seq network analysis
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: regulated by MYB100; MYB31; KN1; FEA4; P1; GRF4 and many other TFs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered lignin composition may affect cell wall properties
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq and RNA-seq integration from published datasets
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native COMT1 promoter used as Y1H bait
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: major node for engineering lignin composition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT1 promoter was one of the most connected in the phenolic GRN. 2. It is a key late lignin-pathway gene controlling conversion between G- and S-lignin. 3. Multiple activating and repressing TF inputs converge on COMT1 in the integrated network.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included here as a core promoter-centered gene in the study although it is a pathway enzyme gene biologically.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor supply for lignin and flavonoid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: early phenylpropanoid gene acting as major regulatory convergence point
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter-centered Y1H; ChIP-qPCR for selected TFs; integrated ChIP-seq/RNA-seq network analysis
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: regulated by P1; KN1; FEA4; ARF34; MYB100 and multiple additional TFs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: modulation likely affects multiple downstream phenolic branches
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq and RNA-seq integration from published datasets
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native 4CL3 promoter used as Y1H bait
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strategic control point for redirecting phenylpropanoid flux
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL3 encodes an early step in the general phenylpropanoid pathway. 2. Its promoter is controlled by multiple TFs and feed-forward loops in the integrated GRN. 3. The study highlights 4CL3 as a major regulatory hub connecting developmental regulators to phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included here as a core promoter-centered gene in the study although it is a pathway enzyme gene biologically.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: late anthocyanin biosynthetic gene with very high promoter connectivity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter-centered Y1H; cooperative Y1H; Y2H for TF-TF interactions
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: R (bHLH1); ZHD14; EREB12; LBD4; NAC37 and many additional TFs
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native Bz1 promoter used as Y1H bait
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promoter and regulators offer leverage for anthocyanin engineering
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bz1 promoter was recognized by a large number of TFs, making it one of the most connected promoters. 2. The study uncovered combinatorial regulation of Bz1 by TF pairs including ZHD14+EREB12 and LBD4+NAC37. 3. The known regulator R was recovered on the Bz1 promoter, consistent with prior work.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included here as a core promoter-centered gene in the study although it is a pathway enzyme gene biologically.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural lignification; specialized metabolite functions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL1; PAL3; PAL4; PAL5; PAL6; PAL8; PAL9 and other phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates PAL4 promoter; likely positive regulation of multiple phenolic biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in feed-forward loop-rich phenolic GRN (general network context)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: high-connectivity regulator recognizing 13 phenolic promoters; acts as activator of phenylpropanoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR validation of 13 interactions; transient transactivation in maize protoplasts with pPAL4::Luc
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes expression of phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes, supporting phenolic metabolite biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: etiolated seedling protoplast system; dark incubation after transfection
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE-defined TSS information; integration with ChIP-seq and RNA-seq from published datasets
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p35S constitutive promoter for effector constructs; PAL4 promoter fused to Luc reporter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate positive regulator for engineering coordinated enhancement of phenylpropanoid pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB65 bound 13 maize phenolic gene regulatory sequences, including seven PAL promoters. 2. ChIP-qPCR confirmed 10 of 13 Y1H interactions in maize protoplasts. 3. p35S::MYB65-GFP strongly activated pPAL4::Luc, indicating MYB65 is a transcriptional activator of phenylpropanoid genes in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Arabidopsis AtMYB58/AtMYB63, known positive regulators of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 24 phenolic gene promoters across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: highest outgoing connectivity in the network, suggesting broad coordination of pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate broad regulator of maize phenolic biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target for coordinated modulation of multiple phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD35 recognized 24 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It showed the highest outgoing connectivity among TFs in the network. 3. Its pattern suggests a role in coordinating multiple branches of phenolic biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function of LBD35 or orthologs in other plants was noted as unknown.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 23 phenolic gene promoters across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: high-connectivity regulator suggesting broad pathway coordination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate broad regulator of maize phenolic biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful candidate for multigene engineering of phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD24 recognized 23 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It belongs to the set of 11 TFs binding 10 or more promoters. 3. Its target profile spans multiple branches of phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous Arabidopsis AtLBD16 functions as a transcriptional activator in lateral root formation; phenolic role was newly implicated here.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 18 phenolic gene promoters across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: high-connectivity regulator suggesting coordination of phenolic pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate regulator of broad phenolic biosynthetic output
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for coordinated engineering of phenolic pathway branches
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD20 recognized 18 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It is among the most connected regulators in the phenolic GRN. 3. Its activity suggests broad control across pathway branches.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions of LBD20 or orthologs in other plants were noted as unknown.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DOF (DNA-binding One Zinc Finger) transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural and defense-related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 15 gene promoters corresponding to phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches; validated examples include HCT10
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; transcriptional direction not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: one of the most connected DOF regulators in the phenolic network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR validation for selected promoter interactions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate broad regulator of maize phenolic biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p35S constitutive promoter for GFP fusion in ChIP assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator to tune multiple branches of phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOF28 recognized 15 phenolic gene promoters spanning phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches. 2. ChIP confirmed at least one Y1H-identified interaction in maize protoplasts. 3. DOF28 is a high-connectivity candidate regulator in the phenolic GRN.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis DOF family members have been linked to flavonoid/lignin regulation, supporting plausibility.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CPP (Cysteine-rich Polycomb-like Protein) family transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural and defense-related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13 promoters across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: broad promoter recognition across all branches suggests coordinated control of phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate broad regulator of maize phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: novel regulator family member for phenolic pathway engineering
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPP8 bound 13 promoters across all branches of the phenolic pathway. 2. This identifies CPP-family involvement in phenolic regulation. 3. CPP8 is among the 11 most connected TFs in the network.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPP family had little prior functional characterization in plants.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural and defense-related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13 promoters corresponding to all branches of phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: nitrogen cellular status/nitrate-related signaling inferred from NLP family biology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: broad promoter binding suggests linkage of nitrogen status with phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate integrator of nitrogen-related signaling and phenolic biosynthetic regulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to coordinate nutrient signaling with phenolic content
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP9 recognized 13 promoters across all phenolic pathway branches. 2. This suggests an unrecognized link between nitrogen status and phenolic biosynthesis. 3. NLP9 is one of the most connected regulators in the phenolic GRN.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text mentions NLP9 in Results although the abstracted list of 11 TFs earlier uses NLP14; NLP9 is the specifically described high-connectivity NLP regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural and defense-related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 12 genes across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: broad promoter recognition across all branches suggests pathway coordination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate broad regulator of phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for reprogramming phenolic pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP63 recognized 12 phenolic gene regulatory regions across all pathway branches. 2. It is among the most connected TFs in the network. 3. Its binding profile supports a role in coordinated phenolic gene regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The abstracted list of 11 TFs earlier uses bZIP13; the Results text specifically describes bZIP63 as the broadly connected bZIP regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural and defense-related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL9; 4CL3; ALDH1; ALDH5; COMT1; F5H1; HCT6; HCT10; FLS1; C2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds multiple lignin and flavonoid pathway promoters; direction not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: controls promoters from general phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate cross-branch regulator of maize phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator for coordinated tuning of lignin and flavonoid composition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H42 bound 10 different phenolic pathway promoters. 2. Its targets include general phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid genes. 3. This places C3H42 among the high-connectivity regulators in the network.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional polarity was reported.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant-environment interaction; structural and defense-related phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 10 phenolic biosynthesis gene promoters; validated examples include Bz2 and PAL5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promoter recognition consistent with positive transcriptional control, but direction broadly not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: one of the 11 regulators recognizing 10 or more phenolic promoters
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: bridges TBP and promoter-bound TFs based on MBF1 family biology
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR validation of Bz2 and PAL5 interactions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: candidate broad regulator/co-regulator of phenolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p35S constitutive promoter for GFP fusion in ChIP assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: novel co-regulator candidate for coordinated phenolic pathway manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MBF1.3 recognized 10 phenolic biosynthesis gene promoters. 2. ChIP confirmed in vivo interactions with Bz2 and PAL5 in maize protoplasts. 3. The study supports MBF1.3 as a direct regulator/co-regulator of phenolic pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MBF1 family is discussed as blurring the distinction between TF and co-regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural cell wall metabolism and other phenolic functions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HCT6; COMT1; additionally ChIP-positive association with 4CL3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promoter binding demonstrated; activation/repression not directly shown
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: validated regulator connected to lignin/general phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: possible indirect recruitment for some promoters such as 4CL3 inferred from Y1H/ChIP discrepancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports regulation of phenolic biosynthetic genes including lignin pathway components
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p35S constitutive promoter for GFP fusion in ChIP assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator for manipulating lignin-related phenolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB100 interacted with HCT6 and COMT1 promoters in Y1H. 2. ChIP-qPCR confirmed these in vivo interactions in maize protoplasts. 3. ChIP also detected association with 4CL3 not found by Y1H, highlighting complementary regulatory discovery.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as an example to compare Y1H and ChIP performance.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 4CL3; HCT10; A1 and additional phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive regulation/activation inferred from known function and integrated GRN
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in feed-forward loops in integrated GRN
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: known regulator of maize phenolic metabolism recovered by Y1H and integrated with ChIP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H identification; supported by prior literature and ChIP-seq integration
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for regulation of specific flavonoid and hydroxycinnamate metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: validated regulator for engineering branch-specific phenolic compounds
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P1, a known maize phenolic regulator, was recovered in the Y1H screens. 2. It recognized nine phenolic promoters in Y1H, with overlap to ChIP-seq targets. 3. P1 participates in integrated feed-forward regulatory motifs controlling phenolic genes such as 4CL3.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Established regulator from prior studies; included as a recovered known controller rather than newly discovered factor.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall formation and mechanical support
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: COMT1 and other lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses lignin biosynthetic genes including COMT1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: known lignin repressor recovered in the phenolic regulatory framework
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces expression of lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered lignin may affect cell wall properties
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for lowering lignin or altering lignin composition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB31 was recovered as a known regulator in the Y1H-derived network. 2. Prior studies cited show it represses lignin biosynthetic genes including COMT1. 3. The integrated network places MYB31 among regulators affecting lignin branch control.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its paralog MYB42 was absent from the AD-TF library.
